name: Tichodromidae
description: Tichodromidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found in high mountain ranges across Europe and Asia. The Wallcreeper is renowned for its stunning crimson and grey wings and its habit of climbing vertical rock faces.

children.name: Tichodroma
children.description: A monotypic genus of unique passerine adapted to climbing on vertical rock surfaces in mountainous regions.

children.name: Tichodroma muraria
children.description: The Wallcreeper is a striking grey bird with brilliant crimson wing patches that flash in flight. It climbs vertical cliffs and rock faces in the Pyrenees, Alps, Himalayas, and other mountain ranges, probing crevices for insects.
